caption: Funnel-routing crowding near the hill (#979): two friendly Cavalry regiments (uid0 west of the hill, uid1 spawning inside the hill's own x-range) are each given an explicit MOVE order to a point just north of the hill, so both round the hill's shorter, west side at the same time. PathField's funnel corner is a pure function of the blocking rect's geometry and each unit's own clearance -- two same-type regiments compute the literal identical waypoint, and since same-team crowding is resolved only by soldier-body collision (not the regiment-level separation, which only pushes ENEMY pairs apart), the two blocks used to jam into each other for hundreds of ticks before crowd pressure eventually broke the tie. Verified directly on this exact scenario: with the fix's own offset temporarily zeroed, uid0 stalls around y~750-810 for hundreds of ticks while uid1 completes its march cleanly -- reproducing the reported deadlock. With the fix (a small deterministic per-uid lane offset breaking the tie before the units ever converge), both regiments complete the same march within about a thousand ticks, tracking cleanly the whole way: the algorithmic defect scan (overlap, blob, shape_residual, facing_whipsaw, misslotted, superphysical_speed) passes all 16 checks for both units, with a comfortable margin on every one -- not a knife's edge. An earlier version of this demo used the default battle's own AI-driven attack orders and a much longer march, which (independent of this fix) surfaced a separate, periodic formation-compression transient during long marches -- not shown here, since it isn't specific to the crowding bug this PR fixes.
